steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence does two things: it raises a possible interpretation (‘fast painting accounts for the Highwaymen's ethereal qualities’) and immediately rejects it (‘tempting but inaccurate’). The correct answer must capture both moves — raising and then refuting an interpretation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘contradicts a claim about a long-standing disagreement within the group’: The text contains no mention of a disagreement among the Highwaymen; this fabricates a conflict not present in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘explains how an artist adapted a style originated by his colleagues’: The underlined portion is about the effect of the technique on the group's aesthetic, not about how any individual developed his style. It also reverses the direction — Hair originated fast painting, not his colleagues. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘considers and rebuts an interpretation of the effect of a painting technique on the perception of the group's work’: This matches precisely: the sentence entertains the interpretation that fast painting caused the ethereal quality, then disproves it with the Roy McLendon counterexample. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘contrasts central artists with peripheral ones’: The text never distinguishes between central and peripheral Highwaymen; McLendon is presented simply as a member of the group, not as marginal to it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
